item.title: What's new — mid-2026
item.description:
Every so often we’ll stop, look up from the workbench, and tell you what flove has been up to. This is the first of those — a devlog, though really it’s just flove thinking out loud in public.

If there’s one thread running through everything below, it’s this: most of the internet is built to hold onto you — your attention, your data, your habit of coming back. flove is quietly trying the opposite. The big moves this stretch were all about making flove something you can simply have and keep — on your own device, working whether or not the network (or we) are around. Less a service that keeps you; more a thing you own. Here’s what that looked like in practice.

item.title: The road so far
item.description:
Before the devlog picks up entry by entry, here’s the whole arc in one place — how flove got from an idea to the thing you can install today.

By the numbers

flove is young and dense. As of this first post:

  • ~2 months of building — first commit 7 May 2026, and it hasn’t stopped.
  • 1,100+ commits on the main line, from a single pair of hands.
  • ~30 apps, and 70-plus single-file builds once you count their tiers and variants, grouped in about ten clusters — browse them all at flove.org/apps.
  • Built as an intense human + AI pairing — on the order of 120 sessions of working side by side with Claude.

Small team, short calendar, a lot of ground. Here’s how it fits together.

item.title: Welcome — and what this blog is for
item.description:
If you’ve landed here, you’ve probably already met flove — or you’re about to. The short version: flove is a bet on a slower internet, one where relating, not extracting, is the point. Not a feed that mines your attention, but a small constellation of tools and ideas built at human scale, to be kept rather than to keep you.

This blog is the workshop window. It’s where we think out loud, announce what ships, and keep an honest record of how flove is made. It has three shelves:

item.title: Hello, flove
item.description:
This is the flove blog — built with Hugo, served static from flove.org/blog/, with zero JavaScript and one small stylesheet.

Posts live in blog-src/content/posts/. Write Markdown, run ./build-blog.sh, commit the generated /blog, and it goes live on the next update-web pass.

slow it · flow it · love it.
